claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 18 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 18 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit in the UK. 

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your situation. Let me start with:

**Do you have any children that you're responsible for?** If yes, could you tell me:
- How many children you have
- Their names (or you can use Child 1, Child 2, etc. if you prefer)
- Their ages

This will help me understand your situation and guide you through the eligibility assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do! I have one child - my son Alex. He's 18 years old and lives at home with me. I wanted to check if I can still claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I can help you check eligibility for Alex.

Since Alex is 18 years old, there are some important considerations. Child Benefit generally continues after age 16 only if the child is in approved education or training.

**Is Alex currently enrolled in any of the following:**
- Full-time non-advanced education (such as A-levels, Scottish Highers, or similar courses at school or college)?
- Approved training programs (such as certain vocational training or apprenticeships that count for Child Benefit)?

Or has he left education/training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is still in education. He's studying for his A-levels at sixth form college.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you! Alex being in full-time education doing A-levels is important for his eligibility.

Now I need to ask a few more questions to complete the assessment:

**When did Alex turn 18, or when will he turn 18?** (I need to know the specific date if possible, or at least which month and year)

This is important because there are specific rules about how long Child Benefit can continue after a young person turns 18 while they're still in education.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He turned 18 in March of this year - March 2024.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now I need to understand when his current course ends:

**When does Alex's A-level course finish?** Specifically, I need to know:
- When is the last day of his course or when do his final exams end?
- Is this before or after the end of August 2024?

This is important because Child Benefit can continue after age 18, but only up until a certain point depending on when the course ends.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: His A-levels finish at the end of June 2024 - that's when his final exams are done. So yes, that's before the end of August.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

**Are you the person who is responsible for Alex?** By this I mean:
- Does Alex live with you?
- Are you the one who is financially responsible for him (providing for his needs, housing, etc.)?

And secondly, **are you currently living in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me here at home, and I'm the one who's responsible for him - I provide for all his needs. And yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for providing all that information. Let me just clarify one final important detail:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?** For example, Alex's other parent or another family member?

This is important because only one person can claim Child Benefit for the same child.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. It's just me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's the situation:

✅ **You can claim Child Benefit for Alex until 31st August 2024.**

Since Alex is 18 and studying A-levels (which is approved full-time non-advanced education), Child Benefit can continue even though he's over 16. The key rule is that for young people in approved education or training, Child Benefit continues until:
- The 31st of August following their 19th birthday, OR
- When they leave their course

**Whichever comes first.**

In Alex's case, since his A-levels finish at the end of June 2024 (before the end of August), your Child Benefit will continue until **31st August 2024**. After that date, Child Benefit will stop because he will have completed his course.

You meet all the other requirements:
- You're responsible for Alex
- He lives with you in the UK
- No one else is claiming for him

If you're not already claiming, you should apply as soon as possible. If you're already receiving Child Benefit for Alex, it should automatically continue until the end of August 2024.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old (turned 18 in March 2024) and is currently studying for his A-levels at sixth form college, which qualifies as full-time non-advanced education. His A-level course finishes at the end of June 2024, which is before the end of August 2024. Under UK Child Benefit rules, Child Benefit can continue for young people aged 16-19 who are in approved education or training. Specifically, Child Benefit continues until the 31st of August following the child's 19th birthday, or until they leave approved education/training, whichever comes first. Since Alex turned 18 in March 2024 and his course ends in June 2024 (before the end of August 2024), Child Benefit can continue until the end of August 2024 (the 31st of August 2024). The claimant is responsible for Alex, he lives with them in the UK, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. Therefore, the claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex until the end of August 2024.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 18-year-old son Alex, who is currently studying A-levels. The benefit can continue until the 31st of August 2024, as Alex is in approved full-time education and his course ends before that date. The claimant is responsible for Alex, they live together in the UK, and no one else is claiming for him.

His A-level course finishes at the end of June 2024, which is before the end of August 2024. Under UK Child Benefit rules, Child Benefit can continue for young people aged 16-19 who are in approved education or training. Specifically, Child Benefit continues until the 31st of August following the child's 19th birthday, or until they leave approved education/training, whichever comes first. Since Alex turned 18 in March 2024 and his course ends in June 2024 (before the end of August 2024), Child Benefit can continue until the end of August 2024 (the 31st of August 2024). The claimant is responsible for Alex, he lives with them in the UK, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. Therefore, the claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex until the end of August 2024.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 18-year-old son Alex, who is currently studying A-levels. The benefit can continue until the 31st of August 2024, as Alex is in approved full-time education and his course ends before that date. The claimant is responsible for Alex, they live together in the UK, and no one else is claiming for him.
